flashcards.back: The value of a digit based on its position in a number. Example: in 345, the 3 has place value 300 (hundreds place).

flashcards.front: What are the place values for whole numbers (right to left)?
flashcards.back: Ones, tens, hundreds, thousands, ten thousands, hundred thousands, millions, etc.

flashcards.front: What are the place values after the decimal point?
What is the place value of 0 in any position?
flashcards.back: Always 0, because 0 × (any power of 10) = 0. But it still serves as a placeholder.
